description: Improve perception and animation planning so that animals/characters are decomposed into articulated sub-parts (head, body, limbs, tail) and the planner generates rotate+pivot joint motion instead of whole-body translate bouncing.

userStories.title: Enhance VLM perception prompt for animal/character sub-part decomposition
userStories.description: As the system, I need the perception prompt to aggressively decompose animals, characters, and creatures into sub-parts (head, body, limbs, tail) so that each limb can be animated independently.
acceptanceCriteria: PERCEPTION_PROMPT in backend/app/routers/perception.py is updated with explicit animal/character decomposition guidance
Prompt includes concrete examples: cat → head/body/front_legs/hind_legs/tail, person → head/torso/left_arm/right_arm/left_leg/right_leg
Prompt states: any creature or character with visible limbs MUST be decomposed into sub-parts even if the pose is compact
Prompt emphasizes: each sub-part must have a pivot at the natural joint (shoulder for front_legs, hip for hind_legs, base for tail)
Prompt emphasizes: child bboxes should tile the parent bbox as completely as possible — no large uncovered gaps
Existing guidance for robots/wings/wheels remains intact (backward compatible)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This is a prompt-only change to perception.py. Do NOT change any Python logic — only the PERCEPTION_PROMPT string and the PERCEPTION_TOOL schema descriptions if needed. Verify by reading the updated prompt and confirming it covers the listed examples.

userStories.title: Add sub-part coverage validation with warning log
userStories.description: As the system, I need a post-perception validation step that warns when the union of child bboxes poorly covers the parent bbox, so bad decompositions are caught early.
acceptanceCriteria: New function _validate_sub_part_coverage(elements: list[Element]) -> None in backend/app/routers/perception.py
For each parent element (elements that have children with matching parent_id), compute the ratio: union-area-of-child-bboxes / parent-bbox-area
When coverage ratio < 0.70, log a WARNING via Python logging: 'Sub-part coverage for {parent_id} is {ratio:.0%}, below 70% threshold'
The function is called in the perceive_elements route AFTER parsing elements, BEFORE returning the response
Validation never raises or blocks — it only logs warnings
Unit test: given parent bbox [0,0,100,100] and two child bboxes [0,0,50,100] and [50,0,50,100] → coverage = 1.0 (no warning)
Unit test: given parent bbox [0,0,100,100] and one child bbox [0,0,30,30] → coverage = 0.09 (warning logged)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Use simple axis-aligned bbox union area calculation. No need for pixel-level accuracy — just compute the union of rectangles. The union area can be approximated by painting onto a 1-bit grid or by using the inclusion-exclusion principle for rectangles.

userStories.title: Enhance planner prompt with articulated motion patterns
userStories.description: As the system, I need the animation planner prompt to understand articulated motion patterns (running, walking, waving, wagging) and generate rotate+pivot timelines for sub-parts instead of whole-body translate bouncing.
acceptanceCriteria: PLANNER_PROMPT in backend/app/routers/plan_animation.py is updated with articulated motion guidance

Prompt includes a 'Motion Pattern Reference' section with concrete timeline templates for: running/walking (alternating leg rotation ±15-25° around hip/shoulder pivot, loop=true), waving/swinging (arm rotation ±20-30° around shoulder pivot), tail wagging (small rotation ±10-15° around tail base pivot)
Prompt states the rule: for sub-parts (elements with parent_id), ALWAYS prefer rotate+pivot over translate — translate on a sub-part detaches it from the parent body
Prompt states: when the user asks for 'running' or 'walking', animate the legs with alternating rotate phases, do NOT translate the parent up and down
Prompt states: each rotate step in a looping oscillation should return to the neutral angle (0°) to avoid drift
Existing planner guidance for whole-object motion (jump, slide) remains intact
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This is a prompt-only change to plan_animation.py. Only modify the PLANNER_PROMPT string. Do NOT change any Python logic, schemas, or tool definitions. The timeline template examples should be written in plain English within the prompt, not as JSON — the LLM will translate them to DSL.

userStories.title: Add yoyo oscillation support to animation DSL and frontend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a yoyo flag in the animation DSL so that oscillating motions (leg swing, tail wag) automatically reverse, instead of requiring 3+ explicit rotate steps.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/app/schemas/animation.py: ElementAnimation model gains yoyo: bool = False
backend/app/routers/plan_animation.py: PLANNER_TOOL schema for report_animation_plan includes yoyo boolean field in the element-level properties

PLANNER_PROMPT updated to mention yoyo: 'For oscillating motions (leg swing, pendulum, tail wag), set yoyo=true and loop=true — the timeline will automatically reverse. This means you only need one rotate step (e.g. rotate +20°) and it will swing back and forth.'
frontend/src/lib/animator.ts: AnimationDSL ElementAnimation interface gains yoyo?: boolean
frontend/src/lib/animator.ts: buildTimeline uses yoyo when creating GSAP sub-timeline: gsap.timeline({ repeat: loop ? -1 : 0, yoyo: yoyo ?? false })
Existing tests still pass (yoyo defaults to false, no behavior change for existing DSL)
New backend test: AnimationPlan with yoyo=true validates without error
New frontend test: buildTimeline with yoyo=true creates a GSAP timeline with yoyo property set
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This touches 4 files: animation.py (schema), plan_animation.py (tool schema + prompt), animator.ts (GSAP mapping), and their respective tests. Keep changes minimal — yoyo is a simple boolean flag that maps directly to GSAP's native yoyo property.

userStories.title: End-to-end articulated cat test with mocked VLM responses
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an integration test that verifies the full perception → segment → plan-animation chain produces articulated leg motion for a cat with prompt '让小猫原地奔跑'.
acceptanceCriteria: New file: backend/tests/e2e/test_articulated_cat.py

Test uses a fixture perception response with parent element 'black_cat' (bbox covering the cat region) and at least 3 children: black_cat.body, black_cat.front_legs, black_cat.hind_legs — each with parent_id='black_cat' and appropriate pivot values
Test uses a fixture plan-animation response where black_cat.front_legs and black_cat.hind_legs have timeline steps of type 'rotate' (not translate) with non-null pivot values
Test asserts: the plan DSL for leg sub-parts uses rotate primitives
Test asserts: the plan DSL for leg sub-parts has loop=true (continuous running motion)
Test asserts: the parent element 'black_cat' either has no translate dy or only a small subtle one (not a large bounce)
Fixture files stored in backend/tests/e2e/fixtures/ as articulated_perception.json and articulated_plan.json
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This test uses MOCKED VLM responses (fixtures) — it does NOT call the real Claude API. The purpose is to verify the data contract: given a well-decomposed perception result, the planner should produce articulated rotate animations. The segment step should be mocked with solid masks (same pattern as existing test_pipeline.py). Focus the assertions on the SHAPE of the DSL, not exact numeric values.
